steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.plantatam: 'Plantatam' is a perfect passive participle, not a finite verb
hints.Arborem: 'Arborem' is a noun meaning 'tree' in the accusative
hints.quidam: 'Quidam' is an indefinite pronoun meaning 'a certain (man)'

steps.reveal: habebat = he was having / he had

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'habet' — 'habebat' has the -ba- imperfect marker
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'habuit' — 'habebat' has the imperfect -ba-

hints.habito, habitare, habitavi, habitatum: These are the parts of 'habitare' (to dwell) — a different verb
hints.abeo, abire, abii, abitum: These are the parts of 'abire' (to go away)
hints.debeo, debere, debui, debitum: These are the parts of 'debere' (to owe) — similar pattern but different verb

hints.present active participle: A present active participle would end in -ans/-ens — 'plantatam' ends in -atam
hints.future active participle: A future active participle would end in -urus/-ura — 'plantatam' is -ata
hints.gerundive: A gerundive would end in -andus/-anda — 'plantatam' is a completed action

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'arbor' — the -em ending marks accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'arbore' — 'Arborem' with -em is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'arboris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'arboris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'arboris' ends in -is

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'ficus' — 'fici' with -i is genitive
hints.dative: Dative singular of 'ficus' (4th decl.) would be 'ficui' — 'fici' is genitive
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'ficu' — 'fici' is genitive singular

hints.nominative: While 'vinea' looks nominative, it follows 'in' indicating location — ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'vineam' — 'vinea' after 'in' (place where) is ablative
hints.vocative: Vocative would look the same but no one is addressing a vineyard here

translation: A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard
